context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements multi-source recommendation scoring with specific weights, category diversity constraints, popular-item fallback, A/B test assignment via MD5 hash, correct A/B metrics and graduation criteria, serendipity slots, and privacy/GDPR opt-out as specified by the personalization skill.

checklist.name: PDP signal weight 2.0
checklist.description: When the current product context (PDP) is used, similar-item scores are multiplied by 2.0 (not 1.0, 1.5, or 3.0)

checklist.name: Recent-views signal weight 1.0
checklist.description: Scores from items similar to recent views are multiplied by 1.0 (no additional multiplier beyond the similarity score itself)

checklist.name: Category affinity signal weight 0.1
checklist.description: Category-affinity-based candidate scores are multiplied by 0.1 (not 0.5, 1.0, or omitted)

checklist.name: Recent views scope: top 10
checklist.description: Only the most recent 10 viewed products are used as seeds when sourcing candidates from recent views (not all views, not 5 or 20)

checklist.name: Category scope: top 5
checklist.description: Only the top 5 categories by affinity score are used when generating category-based candidates (not top 3 or top 10)

checklist.name: Exclude viewed and cart items
checklist.description: The final ranked list filters out products that appear in the user's recentViews AND products currently in the cart

checklist.name: Popular-items fallback
checklist.description: When fewer personalized candidates than the requested limit exist, the result is topped up from a popular-products list

checklist.name: Max 4 items per category diversity
checklist.description: The recommendation result enforces a maximum of 4 items from any single category (not 3 or 5 or unconstrained)

checklist.name: 20% serendipity slots
checklist.description: Approximately 20% of recommendation slots are reserved for serendipity (trending, new arrivals, or items from unexplored categories)

checklist.name: MD5 hash for A/B assignment
checklist.description: The experiment.ts module uses MD5 hashing of userId to assign users to variants deterministically (not random, not SHA-256)

checklist.name: 50/50 experiment split
checklist.description: The A/B assignment splits users 50/50 into 'control' and 'personalized' variants (bucket < 50 = control)

checklist.name: A/B metrics: CVR, AOV, RPV
checklist.description: design-notes.md mentions tracking conversion rate, average order value (AOV), and revenue per visitor as the primary A/B test metrics

checklist.name: Graduation criteria: p<0.05 and 2+ weeks
checklist.description: design-notes.md states that personalization graduates to 100% only when p < 0.05 statistical significance is achieved over at least 2 weeks

checklist.name: Privacy opt-out and data deletion
checklist.description: opt-out.ts implements user opt-out from personalization AND a GDPR-compliant user profile deletion function
